title: Good news
visual_consistency: Keep the same modest office and the same three office characters across all four frames. The employee narrator is a slim young office worker in his late twenties, short dark hair, pale blue shirt, simple tie, expressive worried face that becomes relieved in the final frame. The secretary is a calm adult woman with neat shoulder-length dark hair, wearing a soft grey cardigan at a simple desk. Mr. Harmsworth is the same serious but kind middle-aged boss with greying hair, round glasses, dark jacket, seated behind a plain wooden desk. Keep the office warm, ordinary, child-friendly, and not luxurious.

frames.prompt: A simple friendly office scene: a secretary at a desk gently passing a message to a worried employee that the boss wants to see him. The employee looks uncertain but not frightened, and the office feels modest and ordinary. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.

A nervous employee entering a quiet office while Mr. Harmsworth sits at a desk looking down at papers and not looking up. The atmosphere should be tense but gentle, like a small misunderstanding, not dark or cruel. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.

Inside the same modest office, the serious boss explains bad business while the employee sits anxiously, imagining lightly that many people have already left the company. Show worry through posture and expression, but keep it warm and child-friendly. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.

The boss smiles warmly across the desk and gives unexpected good news to the relieved employee, who looks surprised and happy. The office mood turns bright and friendly, clearly a positive reversal after tension. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.
